Container.Name: statesync
Container.Overview: Keeps Emby and Jellyfin watch states in sync.
Container.Description:
StateSync connects to your Emby and Jellyfin servers via their HTTP and WebSocket APIs. When a user pauses, resumes, or finishes a show on one server, the change is pushed to the other within a few seconds. Supports any number of Emby and Jellyfin servers (the daemon discovers new users automatically).
Open the web UI on port 8754 after install to add servers. API keys are stored in the config volume and masked in the UI.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port the dashboard listens on. Open this in your browser after the container starts.

Config.Name: Config Volume
Config.Description: Folder on the array where config.json is stored. Persists across container updates.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Your local timezone, e.g. America/New_York or Europe/Berlin. Used only for log timestamps.

Config.Name: Log Verbosity
Config.Description: How chatty the logs are. info is normal. Use debug for troubleshooting (very chatty).

Config.Name: Allow HTTP to Servers
Config.Description: Allow plain http:// URLs when talking to your Emby/Jellyfin servers. Default true (LAN-friendly). Set false if your servers use HTTPS.

Config.Name: Dashboard Password
Config.Description: OPTIONAL. Leave blank to let anyone on your LAN use the dashboard. To require a password, paste a token in the format: bearer:YOURTOKEN (generate with: openssl rand -hex 32)
